content:
- INITIAL LEVELS AND LIQUIDITY
This is a short video on Forex, looking at the daily chart of the dollar Index. Shared charts on Twitter with
specific levels. The market did in fact trade down into the sell-side liquidity into a value Gap, in the form of a
buy-side imbalance, sell-side inefficiency (BISI).
- All levels linked to this logic on the daily chart.

- OPENING PRICE AND FAIR VALUE GAP TARGET
On the hourly chart, the opening price on this candle here for the 9th of September 2023. There was a sell
- side liquidity pool with relatively equal lows. The market was expected to:
- Open
- Rally up
- Sell off down into our fair value Gap on the daily chart.

- LONDON SESSION AND BEARISH OUTLOOK
On the 15-minute time frame, buy-side liquidity here and sell-side liquidity here during the London session.
The midnight opening candle had its opening price extended through.
- If bearish, looking for lower prices, meaning it is a risk-on scenario.
- If the dollar rallies, it will take out buy-side or trade into an inefficiency in the form of SIBI or fair value gap.

- NEW YORK OPEN KILLZONE AND SIBI
The market trades above, takes out buy-side liquidity, attacks the sell-side liquidity here. During the New
York open Killzone (7 o'clock to 9 o'clock in the morning New York local time), there was a drop.
- A really nice SIBI (sell-side imbalance, buy-side inefficiency) was observed.
- The line representing the old relatively equal low on the daily chart was significant.
- Retracement did not come back up to the daily old lows nor to the old lows here.

- CONSEQUENT ENCROACHMENT TO DAILY FVG
Support and resistance failed, which is why it is not preferred. However, if using SIBI (sell-side imbalance,
buy-side inefficiency), it trades up to consequent encroachment, which is the midpoint of that gap. It trades
up into that, failing to make a return to old daily lows and old equal lows.
- This is where the algorithm was expected to kick in.
- It did, selling off and trading into the daily fair value gap on the dollar Index.
- It closed the session making the low of the day, turning at noon in the London Close Killzone.

- 2022 INTERNSHIP MODEL AND KILLZONE TURNS
On the five-minute chart, the New York open Killzone starts the day, breaking down from high to low,
showing a short-term shift in market structure. This is the model from the 2022 internship.
- Entry was short here.
- Stop above this high.
- Target equal lows to daily sell-side liquidity pool and the fair value gap.
- The market turned again at noon, as taught in the core content and on this channel.

- BUY SIDE LIQUIDITY AND FAIR VALUE GAP
Moving on to Euro Dollar, the daily chart shows levels shared on Twitter. It is suggested to follow on Twitter
to avoid disjointed video content.
- Buy-side liquidity was identified, including an old high.
- A fair value gap was present.
- The market traded up into it, then consolidated intraday and rallied up into the buy-side liquidity pool.

- ORDER BLOCKS AND IDENTIFYING LIQUIDITY
On the hourly chart, an order block from the eighth was observed. The market dropped down into it and
rallied away. Prior to consolidation, the down-closed candle's opening price was extended through, and the
market started to respect that.
- On the 15-minute time frame, Euro Dollar had a sell-side liquidity pool. While either of two identified pools
could be correct, the preference is for levels where candles are real close to being equal highs or lows. This
skill set will be adopted over time.

- LONDON OPEN KILLZONE AND NEW YORK SESSION SETUP
The market drops down in London open, rallies back up, takes out buy-side, expands, has a small
retracement, and rallies up, creating the high. On a five-minute chart, the London open Killzone (2 o'clock
to 5 o'clock in the morning New York local time) was observed.
- Sell-side was taken.
- Rallies up into 7 A.M., the beginning of the New York open Killzone.
- This rectangle highlights the exact time for a setup to form in Forex for the New York session.

section_title: EUR/USD DAILY RANGE FORMATION AND BULLISH ORDER FLOW
content:
For a New York session setup, the period between the New York open Killzone is crucial. The day is
expected to be completed within this timeframe. The daily range high or low should be forming.
- If it's a bullish trending day, generally between 10 o'clock and noon New York local time is going to create
the opposite end of the range.
- If looking for the low to form in London (as taught), that forms the low of the day.
- If bearish on dollar, it means bullish on euro dollar. The market runs up, shows a small alignment
imbalance, trades down into that with a bullish order block, rallies, retraces back down into the order block,
and then ramps up through buy-side liquidity.

To confirm price targets, anchor Fibonacci to the low up to the high and use a 1.5 standard deviation. This
involves:
- Going to Fibonacci settings and inputting 1.5.
- When dropped on the price, it will yield the exact closing prices, not the wicks.
- This combines time (London close, 10 o'clock to noon New York local time) and price (the fulcrum point).
- When price runs through, standard projections can be applied once the high is taken out (standard
deviation 1, 1.5, 2.5, 3, 3.5), until it meets a time window like the Killzone.

Study how the low of the day was formed in the London open Killzone, the continuation in the New York
open Killzone, and how the high of the day is forming in the London close.
- Homework Assignment: Get to the closing price and opening price with Fibonacci, between the low and
that high, measuring that. Projections should lead right up into the target.
- Forex reviews will not be daily but will be a slow rollout, becoming daily every day starting on February 7th.
